subject: Mathematics
description: apply mathematics to problems arising in everyday life, society, and the workplace

subject: Mathematics
description: use a problem-solving model that incorporates analyzing given information, formulating a plan or strategy, determining a solution, justifying the solution, and evaluating the problem-solving process and the reasonableness of the solution

subject: Mathematics
description: determine the domain and range of a linear function in mathematical problems; determine reasonable domain and range values for real-world situations, both continuous and discrete; and represent domain and range using inequalities

subject: Mathematics
description: write linear equations in two variables in various forms, including y = mx + b, Ax + By = C, and y - y1 = m(x - x1), given one point and the slope and given two points

subject: Mathematics
description: write linear equations in two variables given a table of values, a graph, and a verbal description

subject: Mathematics
description: determine the slope of a line given a table of values, a graph, two points on the line, and an equation written in various forms, including y = mx + b, Ax + By = C, and y - y1 = m(x - x1)

subject: Mathematics
description: calculate the rate of change of a linear function represented tabularly, graphically, or algebraically in context of mathematical and real-world problems

subject: Mathematics
description: graph linear functions on the coordinate plane and identify key features, including x-intercept, y-intercept, zeros, and slope, in mathematical and real-world problems

subject: Mathematics
description: graph the solution set of linear inequalities in two variables on the coordinate plane

subject: Mathematics
description: solve linear equations in one variable, including those for which the application of the distributive property is necessary and for which variables are included on both sides

subject: Mathematics
description: solve linear inequalities in one variable, including those for which the application of the distributive property is necessary and for which variables are included on both sides

subject: Mathematics
description: solve systems of two linear equations with two variables for mathematical and real-world problems

subject: Mathematics
description: determine the domain and range of quadratic functions and represent the domain and range using inequalities

subject: Mathematics
description: write equations of quadratic functions given the vertex and another point on the graph, write the equation in vertex form (f(x) = a(x - h)^2 + k), and rewrite the equation from vertex form to standard form (f(x) = ax^2 + bx + c)

subject: Mathematics
description: graph quadratic functions on the coordinate plane and use the graph to identify key attributes, if possible, including x-intercept, y-intercept, zeros, maximum value, minimum value, vertex, and the equation of the axis of symmetry

subject: Mathematics
description: describe the relationship between the linear factors of quadratic expressions and the zeros of their associated quadratic functions

subject: Mathematics
description: solve quadratic equations having real solutions by factoring, taking square roots, completing the square, and applying the quadratic formula

subject: Mathematics
description: determine the domain and range of exponential functions of the form f(x) = ab^x and represent the domain and range using inequalities

subject: Mathematics
description: interpret the meaning of the values of a and b in exponential functions of the form f(x) = ab^x in real-world problems

subject: Mathematics
description: write exponential functions in the form f(x) = ab^x (where b is a rational number) to describe problems arising from mathematical and real-world situations, including growth and decay

subject: Mathematics
description: add and subtract polynomials of degree one and degree two
